sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.87 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: A system of equations with coefficients in a group$G$is said to be if the matrix composed of the sums of exponents of the unknowns has rank equal to the number of equations. [r]{a)} The Kervaire--Laudenbach Conjecture (KLC)\/: every independent system of equations with coefficients in an arbitrary group$G$has a solution in some overgroup~$$. This is true for every locally residually finite group$G...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
